ref(consumers): Rename parallel -> batched-parallel
body:
Both crons and uptime consumers have a parallel mode where they first
create batches and in parallel execute groups of those batches.

I'll be introducing a new parallel mode for the uptime consumer that
does not do any kind of grouping, so I am renaming this to make it more
clear.

This is aligned with the modes for the occurrence consumer

title: Display symlink target path when failing to open one
Introduce `bun ./index.html`
body: ### What does this PR do? This lets you start a frontend dev server by directly running `.html` files in Bun. ```sh > bun index.html Bun v1.2.3 ready in 0.03 ms ➜ http://localhost:3001/ Press h + Enter to show shortcuts ``` For multi-page apps, you can pass multiple routes explicitly. You can also pass a glob. If you pass only one route, that route is matched as the fallback route so that you can use this with single-page apps that do client-side routing from a single shared entry point. ### How did you verify your code works? There are tests

title: Glob Match Rewrite
body:
### What does this PR do?

<!-- **Please explain what your changes do**, example: -->

Rewrites the glob match implementation (both ascii and non-ascii) by porting [the fast-glob crate](https://github.com/shulaoda/fast-glob)

This fixes #14934 as well as a few other issues I found along the way.
Additionally, based on my local testing it results in a large perf win.

The perf win I am referring to was measured by running each test in `test/js/bun/glob/match.test.ts` 10,000 times each and comparing the total runtime for the current and my new implementation. On my machine I get an average of a little over 200x faster than the current implementation. I'd be happy to upload the code for this comparison to github and share if it is of interest.

I didn't do very much more in depth perf testing because my main focus with the PR was to fix the aforementioned issues with the current implementation.

The high-level overview of the change is instead of keeping a track of a `BraceStack` to deal with braces `{foo,bar}`, it recursively creates new globs for each branch (i.e. `1{foo,bar}2` -> [`1foo2`,`1bar2`]). 
This does mean, however, that `matchImpl` must now take in an allocator parameter in order to create the sub globs. For better performance the implementation basically uses a `std.stackFallback` to stack allocate sub globs less than a predetermined length defined by the `GLOB_STACK_BUF_SIZE` variable in both `src/glob/ascii.zig` and `src/glob/GlobWalker.zig` (currently 64 in both places).

You will notice in the PR that I updated all callsites of `matchImpl` to pass in an instance of `Allocator`. I would appreciate if someone more familiar with the codebase than I would double check each place that I did so to make sure I'm using the allocator that makes the most sense in each context. Please note that in places where an allocator was not available in the context I used `bun.default_allocator` rather than change more function signatures, but I'm happy to change this if desired.

- [x] Code changes

### How did you verify your code works?

<!-- **For code changes, please include automated tests**. Feel free to uncomment the line below -->

I wrote automated tests 

<!-- If JavaScript/TypeScript modules or builtins changed:

- [x] I included a test for the new code, or existing tests cover it

-->

title: Set error code in Node-API functions
body:
### What does this PR do?

This is the part of #14501 that made Node-API functions set information about the last error. All functions are supposed to store their error code (including success) in a place where it can be retrieved by `napi_get_last_error_info`.

In Zig code, I made `NapiStatus` an `enum` and `napi_status` an integer type, and it is intended to use `NapiEnv.setLastError` to convert between them, e.g. `return env.setLastError(.string_expected)` will both store the error code on the environment and return the appropriate integer code so the foreign module knows what happened. `@intFromEnum` should only be used in places where you don't have an `env` available or it'd be incorrect to set the error on it for some reason (e.g. I do that in some `napi_threadsafe_function` APIs that may be called from another thread).

In C++ code, we unfortunately have implicit conversions so we have to be more careful to not return an error code without setting it in the environment. Most code should use the `NAPI_` macros at the top of `napi.cpp`, but some cases may need to explicitly call `napi_set_last_error` instead.

### How did you verify your code works?

I wrote automated tests

- [x] I included a test for the new code, or existing tests cover it
- [x] I ran my tests locally and they pass (`bun-debug test test-file-name.test`)

title: fix(js): better error messages when makeSafe fails
body:
### What does this PR do?
Partial redress of https://github.com/opal/opal/issues/2706. Related to #16259.

`makeSafe` can fail when creating safe iterables if users modify the prototype of `Set` or `Map` before primordials are loaded. This PR adds a better error message for such cases, but does nothing to address the underlying problem. We still need to decide on the best course of action there.

I've added tests.

title: Revert "Remove most static initializers (#19298)"
body:
### What does this PR do?

This reverts #19298. The test is failing and polluting our CI, and I can't measure a performance improvement:

```
$ hyperfine -N --runs 1000 --warmup 50 './bun -v' './bun-canary -v'
Benchmark 1: ./bun -v
  Time (mean ± σ):       2.4 ms ±   0.1 ms    [User: 1.7 ms, System: 0.4 ms]
  Range (min … max):     2.3 ms …   2.9 ms    1000 runs

  Warning: Statistical outliers were detected. Consider re-running this benchmark on a quiet system without any interferences from other programs. It might help to use the '--warmup' or '--prepare' options.

Benchmark 2: ./bun-canary -v
  Time (mean ± σ):       2.4 ms ±   0.1 ms    [User: 1.7 ms, System: 0.4 ms]
  Range (min … max):     2.3 ms …   3.1 ms    1000 runs

  Warning: Statistical outliers were detected. Consider re-running this benchmark on a quiet system without any interferences from other programs. It might help to use the '--warmup' or '--prepare' options.

Summary
  ./bun-canary -v ran
    1.00 ± 0.04 times faster than ./bun -v
```

On #19352 I will figure out why the test is failing and if we can restore this. In the meantime we should just revert it.

### How did you verify your code works?

CI

title: hot module reloading for HTML import development mode
body:
Fixes #16335

DevServer gains a new type of RouteBundle for HTML imports. HTML imports have no server files, but may co-exist alongside server routes. While only SPA-style apps are supported now, the tech supports RSC in the same graph as these HTML route bundles.

Instead of `framework: "react"`, the framework option is currently unspecifiable. To allow React to 'just work', the default framework will automatically configure `react-refresh` if it is installed. If only `react` is installed, then an embedded copy of `react-refresh` is enabled to make that also 'just work'.

This PR also resolves some but not all requirements to use HMR reliably, so I still call it experimental. It is very close though.

title: chore: upgrade zig to 0.14.0
body:
Major causes of changes:

- `std.mem.Allocator` vtable changed (alignment is an enum instead of a `u8`; `remap` function added)
- `std.ArrayList.pop` changed to return an optional, and `std.ArrayList.popOrNull` deleted
- runtime page size
- minor `zig fmt` changes
- `std.ArrayList` initial capacity changed (the places that broke have been changed to not rely on the capacity)

title: Update WebKit
body:
### What does this PR do?

WebKit made data() a private member of WTF::Vector. This commit updates all uses to use the appropriate public APIs:

- Replace .data() with .begin() for most Vector uses
- Replace .data() with .span() when constructing std::span
- Use .span().data() for CString objects
- Replace null checks like !vector.data() with vector.size() == 0

This ensures compatibility with the latest WebKit builds.

title: Handle rejected promises in `EventLoop.autoTick`
body:
### What does this PR do?

Adds a call to `this.global.handleRejectedPromises()` inside `EventLoop.autoTick()`. This fixes a failure exposed on #16855. That PR happened to change `Bun.sleep(0)` to sleep for 0ms instead of 1ms, which made `html-rewriter.test.js` fail because if the `element` callback rejects too quickly, the exception would seem to be handled twice (the `catch` block would run, but the exception would also appear globally as an uncaught rejected promise).

I changed the test to use `await setImmediatePromise()` instead of `await Bun.sleep(0)`, which exposes the problem without depending on any timers changes, and I fixed this issue by adding the `handleRejectedPromises()` call.

### How did you verify your code works?

Ran the `HTMLRewriter` test

title: Introduce `server.abort(request: Request)` to abort an in-flight HTTP/HTTPS request
body:
### What does this PR do?

Introduce `server.abort(request: Request)` to abort an in-flight HTTP/HTTPS request.

This immediately closes the TCP socket.

One subtlety here is we have to make sure that we don't call drain microtasks directly in the abort handler. Instead, we have to go through the event loop enter/exit - so that we don't drain microtasks inside of the .abort() function call itself.

title: fix(flags): Fix bug where concurrent accesses to the flags property could raise a RunTime error
body:
On error the SDK deep copies the flag buffer. If the SDK is receiving flags at the same time, the buffer copy can potentially raise a RunTime error.  To fix this we guard the FlagBuffer with a lock.

Fixes: https://sentry.sentry.io/issues/6286673308/?project=1

Issue 451
body:
# Ticket: Implement Core EVM RPC Wrappers & Basic Reads (Polygon Plugin)

**Ticket Type:** Dev Ticket  
**Priority:** P1  
**Epic:** Implement Polygon Plugin MVP  
**Assignee:** Harsh Modi  
**Reporter:** AI Assistant  
**Dependencies:** plugin-setup ticket (relies on initialized structure and configuration)

## 1. Summary

This ticket has been completed. We successfully established the foundational communication layer with Polygon PoS (L2) and Ethereum (L1) networks via JSON-RPC, leveraging the existing functionalities within the @elizaos/plugin-evm. We identified, adapted, and integrated standard EVM RPC wrappers and client setup logic from the EVM plugin into the Polygon plugin's context, and implemented the initial set of basic Polygon-specific read operations using these adapted wrappers.

## 2. Detailed Description & Completed Tasks

### Identified and Reused EVM Logic:
- Thoroughly reviewed the @elizaos/plugin-evm implementation, particularly its RPC client implementation using viem.
- Identified core service, provider, and utility functions responsible for blockchain interactions.
- Leveraged the EVM plugin's approach to chain configuration and client initialization.

### Adapted & Integrated RPC Client Setup:
- Integrated RPC client setup logic into the PolygonRpcProvider class.
- Successfully implemented dual provider management:
  - L1Provider for Ethereum Mainnet (using ETHEREUM_RPC_URL)
  - L2Provider for Polygon PoS network (using POLYGON_RPC_URL)
- Created clear provider selection logic based on network type (L1/L2).

### Adapted & Integrated RPC Method Wrappers:
- Integrated wrapper functions for standard EVM JSON-RPC methods.
- Refactored these functions to handle dual L1/L2 providers with proper typing.
- Preserved and enhanced error handling with TypeScript type assertions.
- Added caching mechanisms to reduce redundant RPC calls.

### Implemented Basic Read Functions:
- Successfully implemented all required read functionalities:
  - `getCurrentBlockNumber()`: Returns latest block number from Polygon.
  - `getBlockDetails(identifier)`: Returns detailed block information.
  - `getTransactionDetails(txHash)`: Returns transaction details and receipt.
  - `getNativeBalance(address)`: Returns MATIC balance for an address.
  - `getErc20Balance(tokenAddress, accountAddress)`: Returns token balance.
- Added additional utility functions:
  - `getErc20Metadata(tokenAddress)`: Returns token symbol and decimals.
  - `estimateGas(tx)`: Estimates gas for transactions.
  - `getGasPrice()`: Gets current gas price.

### Type Definitions:
- Added comprehensive TypeScript interfaces and types.
- Used `as const` for contract ABIs to ensure proper typing.
- Fixed deep type instantiation issues with appropriate type assertions.

### Testing:
- Created standalone test files that don't depend on @elizaos/core.
- Implemented comprehensive tests for all key functionalities.
- Added proper error handling for expected test failures.

## 3. Acceptance Criteria (All Met)

✅ Core RPC client setup logic successfully integrated and adapted to manage distinct L1 (Ethereum) and L2 (Polygon PoS) providers.

✅ Standard EVM JSON-RPC wrapper functions successfully integrated and functional within the Polygon plugin.

✅ Functions `getCurrentBlockNumber`, `getBlockDetails`, `getTransactionDetails`, `getNativeBalance`, `getErc20Balance` implemented and functional.

✅ `getErc20Balance` successfully retrieves the balance for known ERC20 tokens on Polygon (tested with USDC).

✅ Error handling for RPC communication and invalid inputs is functional.

✅ Code includes relevant type definitions and adheres to project linting/formatting standards.

✅ Tests verify the functionality of the core read operations using the adapted wrappers and dual-provider setup.

## 4. Implementation Details

### Key Components:

1. **PolygonRpcProvider**: Core provider class that manages connections to both L1 and L2 networks.
   - Initializes public and wallet clients for both networks.
   - Provides methods for network-specific operations.
   - Implements caching for frequently accessed data.

2. **PolygonRpcService**: Service layer that exposes provider functionality to the plugin.
   - Extends the Service class from @elizaos/core.
   - Manages provider lifecycle.
   - Provides simplified interfaces for blockchain operations.

3. **Configuration**: Updated to use configurable RPC URLs.
   - Default URLs set to Infura endpoints.
   - Allows overriding via environment variables or runtime settings.

### Technical Improvements:

1. **TypeScript Fixes**:
   - Added `as const` to ERC20 ABI and other contract ABIs.
   - Used type assertions to resolve deep instantiation errors.
   - Fixed sendTransaction method parameter handling.

2. **Performance Optimizations**:
   - Implemented multi-level caching with configurable expiry times.
   - Reduced redundant RPC calls for frequently accessed data.

3. **Error Handling**:
   - Enhanced error messages for better debugging.
   - Added proper error type assertions.
   - Implemented graceful fallbacks for common error scenarios.

## 5. Testing Results

All tests pass successfully, verifying:
- RPC connectivity to both Ethereum and Polygon networks
- Block number and details retrieval
- Transaction details retrieval
- Native token balance checking
- ERC20 token interactions (balance and metadata)
- Gas price estimation

## 6. Next Steps

This implementation provides a solid foundation for subsequent features:
- Staking operations on Polygon
- Bridge operations between L1 and L2
- Governance interactions
- Advanced token operations

title: fix: ensure npm package versions include caret prefix during plugin installation
body:
## Description

This PR includes two major enhancements to the ElizaOS plugin system:

1. **Enhanced plugin loading with direct import strategy for @elizaos packages**
2. **Automatic caret prefix for npm package versions during installation**

## Changes

### 1. Enhanced Plugin Loading (`packages/cli/src/utils/load-plugin.ts`)

Significantly improved the plugin loading system with better support for monorepos and npm packages:

- **Added direct import as the first strategy for `@elizaos/` scoped packages** - This ensures proper loading in monorepo environments with strict exports
- **Implemented workspace-aware node_modules resolution** - Automatically finds the workspace root and resolves modules from there
- **Added npm resolution using Node's native `require.resolve`** - Leverages Node's built-in module resolution algorithm
- **Prioritized import strategies** for better reliability:
  1. Direct import for `@elizaos` packages (best for monorepos)
  2. NPM resolved path using Node's resolution
  3. NPM package.json entry point
  4. Local package.json entry
  5. NPM common dist pattern
  6. Local common dist pattern
  7. Local node_modules root
  8. Workspace node_modules root

### 2. Version Handling Enhancement (`packages/cli/src/utils/package-manager.ts`)

Modified the `executeInstallation` function to automatically add caret (`^`) prefixes to npm package versions:

- Detects plain semantic versions (e.g., `1.0.0`)
- Automatically prepends caret (`^`) to create flexible version ranges (e.g., `^1.0.0`)
- Preserves existing range specifiers (`^`, `~`, `>`, `<`, `=`) without modification
- Excludes GitHub packages from this behavior (they use different syntax)

### 3. Additional Changes

- **Updated tests** to reflect the new version handling behavior
- **Cleaned up `bun.lock`** removing redundant entries

## Problem Solved

### Plugin Loading Issues
- `@elizaos/` scoped packages in monorepos often failed to load due to strict exports
- Module resolution didn't properly handle workspace hierarchies
- No fallback strategies when primary import methods failed

### Version Management Issues
- Exact versions from the plugin registry prevented automatic updates
- Missing bug fixes and compatible feature updates
- Required manual intervention for version updates

## Testing

- All existing tests pass
- Added comprehensive test cases for version handling
- Verified plugin loading in both monorepo and standalone environments

## Example Behavior

### Plugin Loading
```javascript
// Before: @elizaos/plugin-openai might fail in monorepo
// After: Direct import strategy ensures it loads correctly
```

### Version Installation
```javascript
// Before
npm install lodash@4.17.21  // Results in exact version

// After  
npm install lodash@^4.17.21  // Results in flexible version range
```

This PR ensures better reliability for plugin loading in various environments and improves dependency management for ElizaOS plugins.

title: feat(after-5169): implement Zod-based character validation with safe JSON parsing
body:
## Summary

This PR implements comprehensive Zod-based character validation with safe JSON parsing to address the issues outlined in #5166.

## Changes Made

### 🔧 Core Schema Implementation
- **New File**: `packages/core/src/schemas/character.ts` - Comprehensive Zod schema for Character validation
- **Schema Features**:
  - Validates all Character interface fields with proper types
  - UUID validation for id fields
  - Flexible settings and secrets validation
  - Support for complex knowledge, messageExamples, and style configurations
  - Strict mode to reject unknown properties

### 🛡️ Safe JSON Parsing
- **Updated**: `packages/cli/src/commands/start/utils/loader.ts`
- **Improvements**:
  - Replace unsafe `JSON.parse()` with `parseAndValidateCharacter()`
  - Enhanced error handling with detailed validation messages
  - Graceful handling of malformed JSON and invalid character data
  - Preserve existing environment-based settings functionality

### ✅ Comprehensive Testing
- **New File**: `packages/core/src/__tests__/character-validation.test.ts` - Schema validation tests
- **New File**: `packages/cli/tests/unit/utils/loader.test.ts` - Loader function tests
- **Test Coverage**:
  - Valid and invalid character scenarios
  - JSON parsing edge cases
  - Complex data structure validation
  - Error message accuracy
  - Environment settings integration

### 🧹 Code Quality Improvements
- **Fixed**: Removed duplicate test files following project conventions
- **Fixed**: Completed all character loading function updates
- **Fixed**: Proper test file organization in correct directories

## Features

- ✅ **Safe JSON Parsing**: Prevents runtime crashes from malformed JSON
- ✅ **Schema Validation**: Ensures character data matches the expected interface
- ✅ **Detailed Error Messages**: Clear feedback for debugging invalid characters
- ✅ **Backward Compatibility**: Existing character files continue to work
- ✅ **Extra Property Handling**: Unknown properties are ignored gracefully
- ✅ **Database Compatibility**: Schema aligns with database agent table structure

## Breaking Changes

None - this is a non-breaking enhancement that improves reliability while maintaining full backward compatibility.

## Implementation Notes

This PR includes multiple commits that iteratively improved the implementation:

1. **Initial implementation** (`7ead757e4`): Core schema and validation logic
2. **Bug fixes** (`2625c272e`): Completed missing loader changes
3. **Cleanup** (`6d6a1b3c0`): Removed duplicate test files

All changes maintain backward compatibility while significantly improving character validation reliability.

## Test Plan

- [x] All existing tests pass
- [x] New character validation tests cover edge cases
- [x] Loader function tests verify error handling
- [x] Build and lint checks pass
- [x] Manual testing with various character file formats
- [x] No duplicate files or code quality issues

## Related Issues

Resolves #5166

## Example Usage

```typescript
import { validateCharacter, parseAndValidateCharacter } from '@elizaos/core';

// Validate parsed character data
const result = validateCharacter(characterData);
if (result.success) {
  console.log('Valid character:', result.data);
} else {
  console.error('Validation failed:', result.error.message);
}

// Parse and validate JSON string
const jsonResult = parseAndValidateCharacter(jsonString);
if (jsonResult.success) {
  console.log('Character loaded successfully:', jsonResult.data);
}
```

title: Fix: Handle Literal types correctly in Partial streaming mode
body: ## Summary - Fixed ValidationError when using Literal types with Partial in streaming mode - Modified `_make_field_optional` to accept any string for Literal fields during streaming - Properly handles required vs optional Literal fields - Added comprehensive tests and example demonstrating the fix ## Problem When using `Literal` types with `Partial` in streaming mode, validation would fail because partial strings (e.g., "A", "Al", "Ali" while typing "Alice") are not valid literal values. ```python class User(BaseModel): name: Literal["Bob", "Alice", "John"] age: int # This would fail during streaming when name is partially complete stream = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", response_model=Partial[User], stream=True, ) ``` ## Solution Modified the `_make_field_optional` function in `instructor/dsl/partial.py` to handle Literal types specially: - During streaming, required literal fields accept `Union[Literal[...], str]` (no None allowed) - Optional literal fields accept `Optional[Union[Literal[...], str]]` - This allows partial strings to pass validation while streaming - Final validation against the original model ensures invalid literals are still caught ## Important Notes - **Required fields stay required**: Required Literal fields don't accept None during streaming - **Type safety maintained**: Invalid literal values (e.g., "Mary" instead of "Alice") will still fail validation when used with the original model - **Streaming experience**: Smooth streaming without validation errors for partial strings ## Test plan - [x] Added unit tests for Literal types with Partial streaming - [x] Added schema validation tests - [x] Added tests for required vs optional Literal fields - [x] Created comprehensive example in `examples/literal_string_stream/` - [x] All existing tests pass - [x] Manually tested with OpenAI API 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes handling of `Literal` types in `Partial` streaming mode by allowing partial strings, with updates to `_make_field_optional` and comprehensive tests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes `ValidationError` for `Literal` types with `Partial` in streaming mode by allowing partial strings. > - Modifies `_make_field_optional` in `partial.py` to handle `Literal` types, allowing `Union[Literal[...], str]` for required fields and `Optional[Union[Literal[...], str]]` for optional fields. > - **Tests**: > - Adds `test_literal_comprehensive.py` with tests for required and optional `Literal` fields, final validation, and schema generation. > - Updates `test_partial.py` with tests for `Literal` types in streaming scenarios and schema validation. > - **Examples**: > - Adds `literal_string_stream` example demonstrating `Literal` types with streaming in `README.md` and `run.py`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=567-labs%2Finstructor&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 62f933436a5f3dc3f202bc9123adb50896204053. title: fix(cloudflare): resolve paths relative to wrangler.json file location
body:
Fixes #501

This PR fixes the WranglerJson resource to ensure that paths in the generated wrangler.json file are relative to the location of the json file itself, not to the current working directory.

## Changes

- Resolve entrypoint and assets paths to absolute paths relative to cwd
- Convert paths to be relative to the wrangler.json file location using path.relative()
- Handle both props.assets and assets bindings consistently
- Add comprehensive test for path resolution behavior
- Update existing test expectations to match new behavior

## Problem Solved

Before: In monorepo setup, WranglerJson would generate `main: "./packages/backend/src/index.ts"` in wrangler.json located at `packages/backend/wrangler.jsonc`

After: Now correctly generates `main: "./src/index.ts"` relative to the wrangler.json file location

feat(os): memoize exec from file patterns
body:
## Changes

1. Enhanced `ExecProps.memoize` to support file-based memoization:
   - Previously: `memoize?: boolean`
   - Now: `memoize?: boolean | { patterns: string[] }`
   - Patterns can be an array of file names or globs, relative to the given working directory if provided or `process.cwd()` otherwise (uses `glob` from `fs/promises`, compatible with Bun and Node.js >= 22)
2. Added tests verifying the new file pattern functionality:
   - Commands are memoized when files haven't changed
   - Commands are re-executed when files are modified

## Example

```typescript
const result = await Exec("build", {
  command: "npm run build",
  memoize: { patterns: ["src/**/*.ts", "package.json"] }
});
```

title: Feat/stripe price meter support
body:
This PR adds support for Stripe Billing Meters in the Price resource, enabling advanced usage-based billing scenarios.

  What's Changed

  Implementation

  - Added meter property to PriceProps and Price interfaces to associate prices with billing meters
  - Implemented proper validation to ensure meters can only be used with recurring.usageType = 'metered'
  - Correctly placed meter inside the recurring object to match Stripe API structure
  - Added meter to output mapping to expose the associated meter ID

  Tests

  - Added comprehensive test coverage for meter functionality:
    - ✅ Creating a metered price with billing meter
    - ✅ Validation that meter requires metered usage type
    - ✅ Validation that meter requires recurring configuration
  - Removed misleading test about meter updates (Stripe doesn't allow updating recurring fields after price creation)

  Documentation

  - Added "Billing Meters" section to price.md with usage examples
  - Updated JSDoc examples in the Price resource with meter usage

  Code Quality

  - Fixed TypeScript type issues by removing unnecessary as any casts
  - Used proper type extensions for Stripe API parameters

  Example Usage

```typescript
  // Create a meter for tracking API usage
  const apiMeter = await Meter("api-usage-meter", {
    displayName: "API Usage Meter",
    eventName: "api_call_made",
    defaultAggregation: { formula: "sum" },
    customerMapping: {
      type: "by_id",
      eventPayloadKey: "stripe_customer_id"
    },
    valueSettings: { eventPayloadKey: "count" }
  });
```

```typescript
  // Create a price with the meter
  const meteredPrice = await Price("api-usage-price", {
    product: "prod_xyz",
    currency: "usd",
    billingScheme: "tiered",
    tiersMode: "graduated",
    recurring: {
      interval: "month",
      usageType: "metered",  // Required for meter association
      meter: apiMeter.id  // Associate with the meter
    },
    tiers: [
      { upTo: 10000, unitAmountDecimal: "0" },      // Free tier
      { upTo: 25000, unitAmountDecimal: "0.002" },  // $0.002 per call
      { upTo: "inf", flatAmountDecimal: "3000" }    // Cap at $3000
    ]
  });
```

  Testing

  All tests pass:
  ✓ Price Resource > create metered price with billing meter
  ✓ Price Resource > meter validation

  Notes

  - Meters enable automatic usage aggregation without manual usage record creation
  - The meter field is immutable after price creation (like all recurring fields in Stripe)
  - This feature requires Stripe API version that supports billing meters

  ---
  This PR completes the meter support implementation requested in the previous conversation, building on top of the tier support already added.

body: # Add New Gradient Component with Unified Gradient Picker Functionality ## Description This PR implements a complete gradient color picker, allowing users to select and apply gradient colors. It adds a unified gradient component with full editing capabilities, preset gradients, and integration into the existing color picker. Additionally, it resolves an unnecessary animation on the import project button that caused UI distractions. ## Related Issues Closes #2239 – [bug] Unable to select or apply a gradient color ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix - [x] New feature - [ ] Documentation update - [ ] Release - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Other ## Features Added ### Gradient Color Picker - **Gradient Tab**: New tab added to the color picker UI. - **Preset Gradients**: Includes a grid of 21 preset gradients. - **Editor Features**: - Draggable gradient stops - Support for linear, radial, conic, angular, and diamond types - Flip and rotate controls - Add/delete gradient stops - Per-stop color picker integration - Real-time gradient preview and valid CSS output ### Bug Fixes - Removed unnecessary animation from the import button in the dropdown menu. ## Technical Implementation - New `Gradient.tsx` component in `packages/ui` - Gradient updates handled via `use-gradient-update.ts` - Integrated with `backgroundImage` in editor engine - Fully typed with TypeScript - Removed old gradient files and cleaned up imports ## Screenshots (if applicable) https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15618208-cc8c-442a-adf7-c40f0885cb39 ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds a new gradient color picker with full functionality and fixes a UI animation bug. > > - **Gradient Color Picker**: > - Adds `Gradient.tsx` component for gradient color selection with draggable stops, type support, and real-time preview. > - Integrates gradient picker into `color-picker.tsx` with a new "Gradient" tab and preset gradients. > - Implements `use-gradient-update.ts` for handling gradient updates in the editor. > - **Bug Fix**: > - Removes unnecessary animation from the import button in `project-breadcrumb.tsx`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=onlook-dev%2Fonlook&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ca3837198783620ded488c28e5b99f0963044377. title: Add support for tag-based component filtering
body:
## Add Tag-Based Component Filtering

This PR introduces tag-based filtering for FastMCP components (tools, resources, resource templates, and prompts), allowing servers to selectively expose functionality based on configurable include/exclude tag sets.

### Key Features
- **Include Tags**: Only components with matching tags are exposed
- **Exclude Tags**: Components with matching tags are filtered out  
- **Precedence**: Exclude tags take priority over include tags
- **Universal Support**: Works across all component types (tools, resources, templates, prompts)

### Usage
```python
# Only expose components tagged with "public" or "api"
mcp = FastMCP(include_tags={"public", "api"})

# Hide components tagged as "internal" or "deprecated" 
mcp = FastMCP(exclude_tags={"internal", "deprecated"})

@mcp.tool(tags={"public", "utility"})
def my_tool(): ...
```

### Implementation
- Added `include_tags` and `exclude_tags` parameters to `FastMCP` constructor and `Settings`
- Implemented `_should_enable_component()` method with comprehensive filtering logic
- Updated all MCP protocol handlers to respect tag filters
- Added `tags` parameter to component decorators (`@tool`, `@resource`, `@prompt`)

Comprehensive test coverage included for all scenarios and edge cases.

title: Add configurable agent step options: continue and include_context_summary
body:
## Summary

This PR adds two new configuration options for agent steps (`^` prefixed steps) that enhance how CodingAgent (Claude Code) operates within Roast workflows.

### New Options:
1. **`continue: true`** - Maintains the Claude Code session across multiple agent steps, allowing for iterative development
2. **`include_context_summary: true`** - Provides an AI-generated summary of relevant workflow context to the agent

## Key Features

### Intelligent Context Summaries
- Created a new `ContextSummarizer` class that uses LLM to analyze workflow context
- Summaries are tailored to the agent's specific upcoming task
- Only relevant information is included, reducing token usage
- Returns "No relevant information found in the workflow context." when there's nothing relevant

### Session Continuation
- Adds `--continue` flag to Claude Code command when `continue: true` is set
- Enables iterative development workflows where agents build on previous work

## Implementation Details

- **Clean Architecture**: Separate `ContextSummarizer` class follows SOLID principles
- **Backward Compatible**: Both options default to `false`, so existing workflows continue unchanged
- **Well Tested**: Comprehensive test coverage for all new functionality
- **Documented**: Updated AGENT_STEPS.md and created example workflows

## Example Usage

```yaml
steps:
  - ^analyze_code
  - ^implement_fix
  - ^add_tests

implement_fix:
  continue: true  # Continue from where analyze_code left off

add_tests:
  include_context_summary: true  # Get AI-generated context about what was analyzed and fixed
  continue: true  # Also continue the session
```

## Testing

All tests pass. The implementation includes:
- Unit tests for ContextSummarizer
- Unit tests for CodingAgent option handling
- Integration tests showing the features in action
- Example workflows demonstrating usage

## Changelog

Updated CHANGELOG.md with the new features in the Unreleased section.

title: Improve workflow system with bug fixes and optimizations
body:
This PR improves the workflow system with several bug fixes and optimizations:

Overall, fixed mutliple warnings when running tests.

## Changes Made
- **Fix variable getter method redefinition**: Prevent redefining getter methods that already exist in each_step.rb
- **Optimize SQLite state repository operations**: Improve database operations and state management
- **Enhance step executor reporting**: Better error handling and reporting functionality
- **Refactor test suites**: Simplify and improve test maintainability across multiple test files
- **Update interpolator test**: Use simpler expectation syntax for better readability

## Files Modified
- `lib/roast/workflow/each_step.rb`
- `lib/roast/workflow/sqlite_state_repository.rb`
- `lib/roast/workflow/step_executor_with_reporting.rb`
- `test/roast/workflow/interpolator_test.rb`

All existing tests pass, and the refactored test suites are more maintainable and readable.

## Impact
These changes improve the robustness and maintainability of the workflow system while fixing potential bugs with method redefinition.

title: feat(db-structure): deprecate schema.relationships in favor of constraintsToRelationships
body:
## Why is this change needed? The schema currently maintains duplicate data structures for relationships and foreign key constraints, leading to redundancy and potential inconsistencies. This change begins the deprecation of the relationships field in favor of deriving relationships from foreign key constraints. ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? - The constraintsToRelationships utility function implementation and its test coverage - Migration approach - using deprecation notice before full removal - Ensure all existing functionality is preserved while using the new approach ## Testing Verification - Added comprehensive unit tests for constraintsToRelationships function - Verified erd-core and agent packages work correctly with the new approach - All existing tests pass without modification - The amount of edges has not changed since the VRT is through. ## What was done - Add constraintsToRelationships utility function to derive relationships from foreign key constraints - Mark schema.relationships as deprecated - Update erd-core and agent packages to use constraintsToRelationships instead of direct relationships access - Add comprehensive tests for constraintsToRelationships function ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at d4c763f3704397e94a4866ae24cf06e6917bb048 - Deprecate `schema.relationships` field in favor of deriving relationships from constraints - Add `constraintsToRelationships` utility function with comprehensive test coverage - Update erd-core and agent packages to use new constraint-based approach - Remove relationship handling from schema text conversion ## Detailed Changes This is phase 1 of removing the duplicate data structure. The relationships field will be removed entirely in phase 2.

title: feat: add RPC function for transactional schema updates with versioning
body:
## Issue ## Why is this change needed? I want to add RPC function for transactional schema updates with versioning - Introduce `update_building_schema` RPC to atomically insert version and update schema - Replace direct insert/update logic in `createNewVersion` with RPC call - Add Valibot schema validation for RPC response - Grant execution permissions on RPC to `authenticated` and `service_role` - Add pgtap tests to verify RPC behavior, including error handling and patch correctness ## What would you like reviewers to focus on? ## Testing Verification - a `design_sessions` record inserted - a `building_schemas` record inserted - login - get access token ### test test curl command: ``` $ curl -i -X POST 'http://localhost:3001/api/buildingSchemas/versions' --cookie 'sb-localhost-auth-token=base64-eyJ....;' \ -d '{"latestVersionNumber":0,"buildingSchemaId":"9bc93231-6718-4a3e-981f-20cc0cbd7221","patch":[{"op": "add", "path": "/foo", "value": "bar"},{"op":"add","path":"/foo2","value":{}}]}' HTTP/1.1 201 Created x-url-path: /api/buildingSchemas/versions content-type: application/json Date: Thu, 22 May 2025 00:25:57 GMT Connection: keep-alive Keep-Alive: timeout=5 Transfer-Encoding: chunked {"success":true}% ``` then, - ✔️ `building_schemas` table record updated. - ✔️ `schema` is filled ## What was done ### 🤖 Generated by PR Agent at 85f605ce3359e91ca129c827ebad0c3e910cb37e - Introduce transactional RPC for building schema updates with versioning - Adds `update_building_schema` function for atomic schema/version changes - Grants execution permissions to relevant roles - Refactor frontend logic to use new RPC and validate responses - Replaces direct table manipulation with RPC call in `createNewVersion` - Adds Valibot schema validation for RPC responses - Add comprehensive pgtap tests for new RPC - Covers success, error, and edge cases for schema updates ## Detailed Changes

[fix][fn] Record Pulsar Function processing time properly for asynchronous functions
body:
<!--
### Contribution Checklist
  
  - PR title format should be *[type][component] summary*. For details, see *[Guideline - Pulsar PR Naming Convention](https://pulsar.apache.org/contribute/develop-semantic-title/)*. 

  - Fill out the template below to describe the changes contributed by the pull request. That will give reviewers the context they need to do the review.
  
  - Each pull request should address only one issue, not mix up code from multiple issues.
  
  - Each commit in the pull request has a meaningful commit message

  - Once all items of the checklist are addressed, remove the above text and this checklist, leaving only the filled out template below.
-->

<!-- Either this PR fixes an issue, -->


<!-- or this PR is one task of an issue -->

Fixes #23705 

<!-- If the PR belongs to a PIP, please add the PIP link here -->


<!-- Details of when a PIP is required and how the PIP process work, please see: https://github.com/apache/pulsar/blob/master/pip/README.md -->

### Motivation

See #23705 
In the realm of asynchronous processing, precise timing and performance metrics are essential for effective monitoring and optimization. Apache Pulsar Functions, as a distributed compute platform, relies heavily on asynchronous operations to process and transform data streams. However, the current implementation lacks a robust mechanism for accurately capturing and reporting the execution times of these asynchronous functions.


### Modifications
This Pull Request introduces several modifications to the Apache Pulsar Functions project aimed at improving the accuracy and reliability of asynchronous function execution time statistics. The primary goal is to enhance monitoring and analysis capabilities for function performance. Here’s a breakdown of the key changes:

1. Removal of processTimeStart Method: The processTimeStart method in ComponentStatsManager has been removed. This method was previously used to record the start time of asynchronous function execution. The start time is now recorded in the JavaExecutionResult object, providing a more centralized and consistent approach.

2. Modification of JavaInstanceRunnable: The run method in JavaInstanceRunnable has been updated to reflect the removal of processTimeStart. The stats.processTimeStart() call has been deleted, and the stats.processTimeEnd() method now accepts the start time as a parameter to calculate the total execution time.

3. Update to FunctionStatsManager: The processTimeEnd method in FunctionStatsManager has been modified to remove the processTimeStart member variable and accept the start time as a parameter. This allows for accurate calculation of the function’s execution time.

4. Changes to SinkStatsManager and SourceStatsManager: The processTimeEnd methods in SinkStatsManager and SourceStatsManager have been updated to accept the start time as a parameter. However, since these classes do not record processing time, the method bodies remain empty.

5. Addition of startTime in JavaExecutionResult: The JavaExecutionResult class now includes a startTime member variable to store the start time of asynchronous function execution. This allows for accurate calculation of execution time within the handleResult method of JavaInstanceRunnable.

6. Modification of AsyncFuncRequest: The AsyncFuncRequest class in JavaInstance now includes a result member variable of type JavaExecutionResult. This change ensures that the processAsyncResultsInInputOrder method uses the existing JavaExecutionResult object instead of creating a new one, maintaining consistency and avoiding duplication.

7. Use of Same ExecutionResult in Non-asyncPreserveInputOrder Mode: In scenarios where asyncPreserveInputOrderForOutputMessages is disabled, the same executionResult object is now used to avoid unnecessary object creation and potential issues with result assignment.

8. Fix for Result and Exception Handling: Two patches address potential bugs related to result and exception handling in JavaInstance. The processAsyncResultsInInputOrder method now ensures that the result and userException fields of JavaExecutionResult are properly set, improving the reliability of the execution result.

9. Addition of Test Case: A new test case, testAsyncFunctionTime, has been added to verify the accuracy of asynchronous function execution time recording and calculation. This test ensures that the start time recorded in JavaExecutionResult is within an acceptable range of the actual start time.


<!-- Describe the modifications you've done. -->

title: Campaign import/export process
body:
| Q                                      | A
| -------------------------------------- | ---
| Bug fix? (use the a.b branch)          | 🔴
| New feature/enhancement? (use the a.x branch)      | 🟢
| Deprecations?                          | 🔴
| BC breaks? (use the c.x branch)        | 🔴
| Automated tests included?              | 🔴
| User documentation | https://github.com/mautic/user-documentation/pull/361
| Developer documentation | https://github.com/mautic/developer-documentation-new/pull/242

## Description

This proof of concept (PoC) implements the Campaign Library feature, focusing on the export and import of a single campaign entity without involving the UI. The goal is to demonstrate the core functionality of exporting and importing campaign data in a streamlined manner.

---
### 📋 Steps to test this PR:

#### 1. **Exporting Campaigns**
Use the following commands to export campaigns:

- Export campaign data in JSON format only:
  ```bash
  ddev exec bin/console  mautic:entity:export --entity=campaign --id=1,2,3 --json-only
  ```

- Export campaign data as a ZIP file:
  ```bash
  ddev exec bin/console  mautic:entity:export --entity=campaign --id=1,2,3 --zip-file
  ```

- Export campaign data as a JSON file:
  ```bash
  ddev exec bin/console  mautic:entity:export --entity=campaign --id=1,2,3 --json-file
  ```
- File name: campaign_data.zip, campaign_data.json

The output files will be stored in the Docker container's temporary directory (`/tmp`). To access these files on your system, use the following commands to copy them to a local directory:

```bash
docker cp ddev-mautic-web:/tmp/campaign_data.zip ../mautic/campaign_data.zip
docker cp ddev-mautic-web:/tmp/campaign_data.json ../mautic/campaign_data.json
```

#### 2. **Importing Campaigns**
To import the exported campaign data, use the following command:

```bash
ddev exec bin/console mautic:entity:import --entity=campaign --file=campaign_data.zip --user=1
```

**Note:** Before running the import command, the required files must be copied to the Docker container's temporary directory. Use the following commands to copy the files into the container:

```bash
docker cp ./campaign_data.zip ddev-mautic-web:/tmp/campaign_data.zip
docker cp ./campaign_data.json ddev-mautic-web:/tmp/campaign_data.json
```

#### 3. **Verification**
- Verify that the campaign data is correctly exported by inspecting the JSON and ZIP files in the system directory.
- Ensure that the imported campaign data is successfully reflected in Mautic.

### 🚀 Expected Results:
- Export commands generate the appropriate files in the Docker container's temporary directory.
- Import commands successfully import the exported campaign data into the system.
- No errors are encountered during the export or import process.

### 🚀 Jira Ticket:
https://mautic.atlassian.net/browse/CL-34

title: Add triangular maze type implementation
body:
## Summary
- Adds a new triangular maze type as option 6 in the maze generator
- Implements stepped triangular shape where each row has one fewer cell than the row below
- Follows the existing rectangular maze pattern for connections and boundary handling
- Includes proper perimeter walls forming a complete triangular boundary

## Implementation Details
- **Structure**: Top row has maximum cells (size), each subsequent row has one fewer cell
- **Connections**: Horizontal connections within rows, vertical connections between adjacent rows
- **Boundaries**: Complete perimeter with left/right vertical walls, top/bottom horizontal walls, and diagonal connecting walls
- **Entry/Exit**: Entry at top-left corner (vertex 0), exit at bottom single cell
- **Integration**: Added to main.cpp menu system and makefile build process

## Test Plan
- [x] Compiles successfully with existing build system
- [x] Generates valid maze structures without infinite loops
- [x] Produces proper triangular boundary shape
- [x] Works with all existing maze generation algorithms (DFS, BFS, Kruskal, Prim, Loop-erased random walk)
- [x] Handles various sizes (tested from 4 to 15 rows)
- [x] Maintains proper maze properties (spanning tree, single path between cells)

## Files Changed
- `src/main.cpp`: Added triangular maze option and menu entry
- `src/makefile`: Added triangular maze object to build process  
- `src/mazetypes/triangularmaze.h`: Header file with class definition
- `src/mazetypes/triangularmaze.cpp`: Implementation following existing patterns

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

title: version 2.1.2
Harden `Wget`, add `LimitedInputStream`, and modernise the test-suite
body:
### Pull Request — **Harden `Wget`, add `LimitedInputStream`, and modernise the test-suite** This series of eight commits turns the one-liner `Wget.url(…)` helper into a small, well-tested, security-oriented component: | ✔ | Area | Summary | | ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | **API** | *Builder pattern, injectable seams* — `ConnectionProvider` & `CharsetDetector` can be stubbed, so callers and tests stay offline and deterministic. | | | **Security** | *Hard byte cap* via the new **`LimitedInputStream`** (package-private). EOF vs. overflow is now unambiguous. | | | **Resilience** | Full guard-rail set:
• URL-length check (2 048)
• Scheme allow-list (http/https)
• Negative/zero budget handling
• Time-outs applied to the default connection | | | **Performance** | Streamed `BufferedReader` read with 4 KiB buffer (commit 2/8). | | | **Docs & Style** | Javadoc everywhere, uniform headers, explanatory commit messages. | | | **Tests** | 40+ assertions across *smoke* and *edge-case* suites, covering concurrency, misbehaving collaborators, EOF semantics, and reflection-verified time-outs. All green on Java 8+ (`mvn test` ≈ 120 ms). | | --- #### 🚀 What’s new? * **`LimitedInputStream`** * Drop-in, zero-dependency wrapper that enforces a byte budget.
• returns `-1` when both wrapper *and* source hit EOF. • throws `IOException` (“Size limit exceeded”) if the source still has data. * **`Wget` refactor** * **Builder API** with sane defaults, optional overrides. * Default provider applies `connectTimeout` & `readTimeout` (tests prove this). * Charset auto-detection hook; null-safe UTF-8 fallback. * Buffered 4 KiB char copying (faster than single-char loop). * Explicit URL-length guard and scheme allow-list. * Negative `maxResponseBytes` rejected early. * **Formatting / documentation** * Consistent 80-column Javadoc paragraphs, ordered imports, UTF-8 header dashes, explicit section rulers. --- #### 🔍 Test coverage highlights * **Happy-path**: body read, custom charset, builder façade. * **Edge-cases**: * zero, exact, and overflowing budgets * provider & appendable exceptions * misbehaving `CharsetDetector` * unknown `Content-Length`, never-ending stream * reflection-verified connect/read time-outs * 20-thread re-entrancy stress * **Regression fix**: correct appendable-exception path (commit 3/8). --- #### ⚠️ Breaking or behavioural changes | Old | New | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `Wget.url(String, StringBuilder)` silently followed redirects, no length guard, read all bytes. | Now enforces byte cap (default 10 MiB), **no redirects**, URL length ≤ 2 048, and throws on overflow. | | Single static method, hard to stub. | Builder + injectable seams; default façade still present for back-compat. | | No time-outs. | 10 s **connect/read** defaults, configurable per builder. | Applications that relied on unlimited downloads or redirects need to opt-in by supplying a custom `ConnectionProvider`. --- #### 🧩 How to migrate ```java // Old one-liner StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); Wget.url("https://example.com", sb); // New preferred style StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); new Wget.Builder() .connectTimeoutMs(5_000) .readTimeoutMs(5_000) .maxResponseBytes(2L << 20) // 2 MiB .build() .fetch("https://example.com", sb); ``` The original static façade still works, but now benefits from the security defaults.

title: Trim obsolete test and polish Javadoc for core top level package
body:
## Why this matters
* The deleted test duplicated coverage already provided by `ChronicleInitTest`
* `core` lacked a package-level description explaining its off-heap memory model.  
* Recent Javadoc linting raised 42 warnings on Chronicle **master** (mis-tagged params, HTML `<` char, missing summary lines). These break `mvn -javadoc:aggregate` when `-Werror` is enabled.

## What is in here
| Change-set | Area | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| **984f5bc** | **tests** | Remove `ChronicleInitRunnableTest` (now redundant). |
| **67deefb** | docs | Add `package-info.java`; drop stale `@since` tag. |
| **d9daefa** | docs | Rewrite method JavaDocs in `Jvm`, `Maths`, `Memory`, `OS`, `Histogram`. *All first sentences now fit 120 chars and use imperative mood.* |
| **b1ecaf0** | docs | Escape “≤” as `<=` to silence Javadoc HTML5 parser. |
| **4bd3fac** | docs | Minor wording tweaks in `StackTrace` and second pass over `Histogram`. |

## Impact
* **Functionality:** none – code paths unchanged.  
* **Docs:** Javadoc generation is warning-free (`mvn -q javadoc:test-javadoc`).  
* **Build time:** –4 s on GitHub Actions (Linux x64, Temurin 21).

title: Improvements to the documentaton for core.io package
body:
This pull request includes several key updates to the Chronicle-Core codebase, focused on enhancing Javadoc clarity, improving resource lifecycle documentation, and refining exception handling. Additionally, several optimisations have been made to code consistency and performance monitoring, especially regarding reference counting and background resource management.

**Key Changes:**

1. **Suppression of Warnings for Busy Waits:**
   - Added `@SuppressWarnings("BusyWait")` to methods using busy-wait loops (`Thread.sleep(1)`) in classes like `AbstractCloseable`, `BackgroundResourceReleaser`, and `TracingReferenceCounted` to address IDE warnings.

2. **Improved Javadoc on Resource Lifecycle and Reference Counting:**
   - Enhanced the Javadoc for resource management and reference counting to provide more context about how resources are acquired, shared, and eventually released, with a focus on how `AbstractCloseableReferenceCounted` and related classes handle resource cleanup in a background thread.
   - Added lifecycle examples for `ReferenceCounted` to clarify how reference-counting operations (`reserve`, `release`, and `releaseLast`) work and how background releasing via `BackgroundResourceReleaser` is integrated.
   - Updated the description for `BackgroundResourceReleaser` to provide more explicit instructions on its use in managing resources asynchronously.

3. **Clarification of Threading and Validation Interfaces:**
   - Refined documentation for interfaces such as `SingleThreadedChecked` and `Validatable`, providing clearer examples of their usage and emphasising the importance of validation in the context of object serialisation.
   - Improved the explanation of the `Syncable` interface for synchronising data with backing stores.

4. **Exception Overview Documentation:**
   - Added a new section to the documentation outlining the key exceptions used in Chronicle-Core, such as `ClosedIllegalStateException`, `ClosedIORuntimeException`, and `ThreadingIllegalStateException`. Each exception is explained with a typical usage example.

5. **Refinement of `ReferenceCounted` Lifecycle Javadoc:**
   - Updated the Javadoc for `ReferenceCounted` and related classes to provide a more detailed overview of their lifecycle, including the handling of reference counting and clean-up in both foreground and background contexts.
   - Removed redundant sections to streamline the explanation of reference counting methods.

6. **Miscellaneous Code Cleanups:**
   - Refined error handling in `BackgroundResourceReleaserTest` for more consistent test results across environments.

**Additional Notes:**
- These updates aim to provide more precise and comprehensive documentation for developers using Chronicle-Core, particularly in understanding resource lifecycle management, reference counting, and threading models.
- The exception overview section adds a new reference for developers to quickly understand how to handle different runtime errors related to resource management.
- The changes also address IDE warnings and enhance the overall stability of resource handling, particularly in concurrent environments.

title: Improve annotation Javadoc for core.annotation
body:
This PR refactors the **Chronicle Core** annotation codebase in three incremental commits.  
Together they **remove redundant test code** and **significantly enrich the Javadoc** for every annotation in `net.openhft.chronicle.core.annotation`, providing clear, consistent and tool-friendly documentation.

---

## Remove unused test

* **Deleted:** `ScopeConfinedTest.java` (49 lines)  
  *The test neither asserted library behaviour nor exercised production code. Removing it shrinks the test-suite surface and eliminates maintenance noise.*

---

## Improve annotation Javadoc (#739)

* **Added ~200 Javadoc lines, removed legacy boiler-plate.**
* **Key themes**
  * First-sentence definitions: **“`@X` indicates …`”** for instant context.
  * *Retention and effect* paragraphs clarifying compile-/run-time visibility.
  * Minimal usage snippets for every annotation.
  * Cross-links (`@see`) so related annotations discover each other.
  * British spelling with US technical keywords (`synchronized`, etc.).

---

## Enhance annotation Javadoc (#740)

* **Follow-up polish** driven by review feedback:
  * Extra examples, limitation notes and small API tables for the sign/range annotations.
  * Deeper rationale for `@DontChain`, `@ScopeConfined`, `@SingleThreaded`, `@UsedViaReflection`.
  * Extended package-level overview (lists every annotation, design goals, author & since tags).
  * Clarified `ChronicleFeature` semantics and `TargetMajorVersion` precedence.
  * Added IDE / ProGuard hints where reflection is involved.

No functional behaviour is altered; **CI should remain green**.

title: Control tracking of non cleaning threads via system property fixes #273
body:
### Summary

This PR is a **full refactor of `CleaningThreadLocal`** that adds opt-in orphan-tracking, modernises the API, tightens safety contracts and expands the test-suite.

### New functionality

* **Global & per-instance orphan-tracking switches**

  * `-Ddisable.ctl.orphan.tracking` → hard-off in prod
  * Assertions (`-ea`) → tracking on by default for debugging
  * New package-private ctor flag to force *on/off* in tests

* **Static factory helpers**

  * `withCloseQuietly(...)`
  * `withCleanup(...)`
  * `withCleanup(..., Function<T,T> getWrapper)`

* **Lazy, best-effort orphan collection**

  * `cleanupNonCleaningThreads()` now delegates to an internal `doCleanup…` helper and returns early when nothing is tracked.
  * Tracking map is created **only when enabled**, keeping the fast path allocation-free.

---

### Implementation & API refinements

* Added `DISABLE_CTL_ORPHAN_TRACKING` constant and richer state fields (`trackNonCleaningThreads`, lazily-initialised `nonCleaningThreadValues`).
* Constructors now enforce non-null contracts via `Objects.requireNonNull` and annotate parameters/fields with JetBrains `@NotNull/@Nullable`.
* `cleanup(T)` is idempotent, swallows/logs user exceptions and is publicly exposed for clarity.
* Overhauled `toString()` for easier diagnostics.
* Internal cleanup loop rewritten for clearer intent and reduced contention.

### Documentation & metadata

* Replaced brief comment with a **detailed, task-oriented Javadoc** (usage, tuning options, launch snippets).
* Updated copyright header to *Chronicle Software 2016-2025*.

### Tests

* **New `CleaningThreadLocalOrphanPruningTest`** – parameterised test that proves the new tracking flag’s behaviour across 128 orphaned threads.
* `CleaningThreadLocalTest` updated to use the new factory methods and verify thread-safety/cleanup paths.

### Compatibility notes

* No behavioural change when orphan tracking is disabled (the default in production).

title: dev->main

title: dev -> main (March 18, 2025)
body:
### **PR Type** Enhancement, Tests, Documentation, Bug fix, Configuration changes, Dependencies ___ ### **Description** - Introduced new integrations for Algolia and Unstructured.io, including setup, arguments, and schema updates. - Enhanced validation, error handling, and logging across multiple modules. - Added extensive tests for new utilities, integrations, and workflows, covering edge cases and diverse scenarios. - Updated and improved documentation for integrations, tutorials, and system architecture. - Fixed issues with database migrations and continuous aggregate views. - Enhanced configuration files with health checks, resource limits, and security improvements. - Added dependencies for new integrations and updated project dependencies. ___ ### **Changes walkthrough** 📝
Relevant files

title: Developers and costs check
body:
### **PR Type** Enhancement, Tests ___ ### **Description** - Add usage cost monthly aggregation and view in database - New migrations for monthly cost tracking and developer view - Implement usage cost limit middleware for free tier users - Middleware blocks requests if cost limit exceeded or account inactive - Paid users bypass cost limit check - Add and enhance usage cost calculation and fallback logic - Average cost fallback for unknown models - Expose usage cost query in API - Add comprehensive tests for usage cost and middleware - Test cost limit, paid tag, error handling, and edge cases ___ ### **Changes walkthrough** 📝
Relevant files
Enhancement
8 files
developer_id.py
Remove developer active check from dependency                       
+1/-12

Add free tier cost limit environment variable                       
+5/-0     
__init__.py
Export get_usage_cost in usage queries                                     
+2/-0     
create_usage_record.py
Add average cost fallback for unknown models                         
+40/-2

Add get_usage_cost query for developer monthly cost           
+52/-0   
web.py
Add usage cost limit middleware for free users                     
+84/-1

Add migration to drop monthly usage cost aggregates/views

Add migration for monthly usage cost aggregation and view
 +34/-0   
Formatting
1 files

+1/-1     
Tests
4 files

Remove dependency test for inactive developer                       
+0/-39   
test_middleware.py
Add comprehensive tests for usage cost middleware               
+464/-0 
test_usage_cost.py
Add tests for usage cost query and aggregation                     
+276/-0 
test_usage_tracking.py
Update tests for fallback cost calculation logic                 
+11/-3   
___ >
Need help?
  • Type /help how to ... in the comments thread for any questions about Qodo Merge usage.
  • Check out the documentation for more information.
    • ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds monthly usage cost tracking, middleware for cost limit enforcement, and comprehensive tests for free-tier users. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds monthly usage cost aggregation and view in the database with migrations `000038_usage_cost_monthly.up.sql` and `000038_usage_cost_monthly.down.sql`. > - Implements `usage_check_middleware` in `web.py` to block requests for free-tier users exceeding cost limits or inactive accounts. > - Paid users bypass cost checks. > - **Models**: > - Adds `get_usage_cost` query in `get_user_cost.py` to retrieve developer monthly costs. > - Updates `create_usage_record.py` to include average cost fallback for unknown models. > - **Environment**: > - Adds `free_tier_cost_limit` environment variable in `env.py`. > - **Tests**: > - Adds tests for usage cost and middleware in `test_usage_cost.py`, `test_middleware.py`, and `test_usage_tracking.py`. > - Removes inactive developer dependency test in `test_developer_queries.py`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=julep-ai%2Fjulep&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 03173f177842272ce22c1d5dad15d7567edabe79. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/julep-ai/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: F/rag-improv: Misc RAG Improvements

### **User description** --- # EntelligenceAI PR Summary **Purpose**: - Enhance document search precision, retrieval, and session management. **Changes**: - **Enhancement**: Introduced new search classes for diversified search modes and precision; modified `recall_options` to include these classes; added `lambda_mult` for message gathering. - **Refactor**: Removed `mmr_strength` from `BaseDocSearchRequest`, reintroduced in new search classes. - **New Feature**: Added `default_system_template` for flexible agent configuration. **Impact**: - Improved document search precision, flexible system configurations, and efficient information retrieval enhance user experience. ___ ### **PR Type** Enhancement ___ ### **Description** - Introduced new document search models (`BaseDocSearch`, `HybridDocSearch`, `TextOnlyDocSearch`, `VectorDocSearch`) and their update variants for diversified search modes and precision. - Enhanced session creation and update payloads with new recall options, improving flexibility and configuration. - Added `default_system_template` field for flexible agent and task configurations. - Integrated advanced recall options (`limit`, `confidence`, `metadata_filter`, `alpha`, `mmr_strength`) into message gathering logic and search behavior. - Updated schemas and models to replace `RecallOptions` with new search models, improving modularity and precision. - Improved document search request models with new parameters like `lang` and `mmr_strength`. ___ ### **Changes walkthrough** 📝

title: feat(agents-api): treat leaf nodes as fstrings
body: > [!IMPORTANT] > Modify `_recursive_evaluate` to treat leaf nodes as f-strings and update tests to verify this behavior. > > - **Behavior**: > - Modify `_recursive_evaluate` in `base_evaluate.py` to treat leaf nodes as f-strings by wrapping them with `f'''{expr}'''`. > - Handles expressions without a dollar sign by evaluating them as f-strings. > - **Tests**: > - Add test case in `test_base_evaluate.py` to verify evaluation of expressions without a dollar sign as f-strings. > - Ensure existing tests cover new behavior for expressions with and without dollar signs. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=julep-ai%2Fjulep&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 00360ebfe61af31a50f27d48aec63f673991dbff. title: GPT Targeting: Add warning when setting the same targeting on multiple slots
body:
## Type of change
<!-- Remove items that don't apply and/or select an item by changing [ ] to [x] -->
- [x ] Bugfix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] New bidder adapter  <!--  IMPORTANT: also submit your bidder parameter documentation as noted in https://docs.prebid.org/dev-docs/bidder-adaptor.html#submitting-your-adapter -->
- [ ] Updated bidder adapter  <!--  IMPORTANT: (1) consider whether you need to upgrade your bidder parameter documentation in https://github.com/prebid/prebid.github.io/tree/master/dev-docs/bidders and (2) if you have a Prebid Server adapter, please consider whether that should be updated as well. --> 
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ x] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes

- [ x] Does this change affect user-facing APIs or examples documented on http://prebid.org?
- [ ] Other

## Description of change
Added a warning when pbjs is setting the same targeting on multiple slots. Updated the unit tests in targeting.js and pbjs_api_spec.js make this behavior clearer. 

Alternative considered was to change the behavior to match the [docs for setTargetingForGPTAsync](https://docs.prebid.org/dev-docs/publisher-api-reference/setTargetingForGPTAsync.html) - which currently say it will set on the first matching slot. Chose not to do this because it could cause unexpected behavior for publisher integrations relying on the current behavior, and it is not obvious that the first slot defined is the one that should be targeted. Open to changing this in the future if there is a safe path. More info in comments + Google doc in https://github.com/prebid/Prebid.js/issues/13060

<!-- Describe the change proposed in this pull request -->
As part of our ongoing efforts to prioritize development resources and align with current strategic goals, we have decided to pause further development for PAAPI requests.
This decision does not reflect a lack of interest or commitment to the feature, but rather a temporary shift in focus. PAAPI support is being parked at this time, and while it will no longer receive updates or enhancements in the near term, we remain open to revisiting it as priorities evolve.

title: Vast tracker library: fix vastTracker url to have updated cpm
body:
## Type of change
- [x] Bugfix

## Description of change

- The current execution sequence places the **Vast Tracker addition** before the **`bidCpmAdjustment`** occurs. This results in the Vast Tracker receiving the **original bid price**, not the **adjusted bid price (`cpmAdjusted`)**.
- As a result, the **Vast Tracker pixel** logs incorrect bid prices, leading to potential discrepancies in **revenue reporting** and **auction accuracy**.
 
### **Key Changes:**

- **Repositioning the Vast Tracker Addition**: The Vast Tracker pixel addition will now occur **before the `addVideoBidCache Hook`**, rather than before the `bidCpmAdjustment` step. This ensures that the Vast Tracker receives the **updated bid price (`cpmAdjusted`)** rather than the pre-adjustment value.
- This adjustment guarantees that the Vast Tracker correctly logs the **final adjusted bid price** for accurate revenue tracking.

- [ ] Updated bidder adapter  <!--  IMPORTANT: (1) consider whether you need to upgrade your bidder parameter documentation in https://github.com/prebid/prebid.github.io/tree/master/dev-docs/bidders and (2) if you have a Prebid Server adapter, please consider whether that should be updated as well. --> 
- [ ] Code style update (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes, no api changes)
- [ ] Build related changes
- [ ] CI related changes

- [ ] Does this change affect user-facing APIs or examples documented on http://prebid.org?
- [ ] Other

## Description of change
<!-- Describe the change proposed in this pull request -->

Adds the `BEFORE_PBS_HTTP ` event to the PBS adapter. This allows modifications like compressing the payload/body.

Example use case of using it for lz4 compression:
```js
pbjs.onEvent('beforePBSHttp', function(event) {
    const requestCompressed = compress(event.requestJson);
    if (requestCompressed) {
        event.customHeaders['Content-Encoding'] = 'lz4';
        event.requestJson = requestCompressed;
    }
});
```

title: PBjs Core : fix creation of per-bidder syncOptions
body:
## Type of change
<!-- Remove items that don't apply and/or select an item by changing [ ] to [x] -->
- [x] Bugfix

## Description of change
This PR relates to issue https://github.com/prebid/Prebid.js/issues/12613 and alters the way syncOptions are sent to bidder by registerSyncInner method.

Instead of checking filterConfig properties, ACTIVITY_SYNC_USER is checked for each bidder, and for both sync types   (iframe and image). This are the same permissions that are later verified by the publicApi.registerSync method.

title: priceFloors & PBS adapter: support mediaType and size specific floors
body:
## Type of change
<!-- Remove items that don't apply and/or select an item by changing [ ] to [x] -->
- [x] Feature

## Description of change

 - Updated priceFloors to pass a 3rd argument `{size, mediaType}` to `inverseCpmAdjustment`
 - Updated ORTB converter to ask for specific floors for each mediatype and banner size. If these are different from `imp.bidfloor`, they're set to `ext.bidfloor` & `ext.bidfloorcur` under `native`/`video`/`banner` and each format in `banner.format`.
 - Updated the PBS adapter to aggregate granular floors in a similar way to `imp.bidfloor` (in case floors are different across bidders).

## Other information

Closes https://github.com/prebid/Prebid.js/issues/12308

title: Targeting.js: Fixed Slot Targeting Bug when RTD is Enabled
body:
## Type of Change
- [x] Bug Fix

## Description of Change
Currently, when RTD is enabled, `pbjs.setTargetingForGPTAsync(adUnitCode)` is setting targeting for all adUnitCodes which have been auction by prebid.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable some RTD provider
2. Do `pbjs.requestBids(adUnit1)` and `pbjs.setTargetingForGPTAsync(adUnit1)`. Here prebid will set targeting for adUnit1
3. Post auction 1 end, do `pbjs.requestBids(adUnit2)` and `pbjs.setTargetingForGPTAsync(adUnit2)`. **Here prebid will set targeting for adUnit1 and adUnit2. Even though adUnit1 was not passed in `pbjs.setTargetingForGPTAsync`**

### Test Page to Reproduce the Issue

Page: https://jsfiddle.net/7bxdqohw/9/

1. Auction 1 occurs for adUnit `div-test-1`, post which correct targeting is set. Below are the targeting for all slots on page:

```javascript
{
  div-test-1: {
    hb_adid: ["4aa111d5cbd6be"],
    hb_adid_medianet: ["4aa111d5cbd6be"],
    hb_adomain: [],
    hb_bidder: ["medianet"],
    hb_bidder_medianet: ["medianet"],
    hb_crid: [],
    hb_crid_medianet: [],
    hb_format: ["banner"],
    hb_format_medianet: ["banner"],
    hb_pb: ["10.00"],
    hb_pb_medianet: ["10.00"],
    hb_size: ["300x250"],
    hb_size_medianet: ["300x250"],
    hb_source: [],
    hb_source_medianet: [],
    mnadc: ["div-test-1"]
  },
  div-test-2: {}
}
   ```
2. Post auction 1 ends, auction 2 occurs for adUnit `div-test-2`, post which correct slot targeting is set for `div-test-2` but targeting for `div-test-1` is also updated, with only the targeting provided by **RTD** provider in first auction, and **bidderSettings** targetings are made null. Below are the targeting for all slots on page

```javascript
{
  div-test-1: {
    hb_adid: [],
    hb_adid_medianet: [],
    hb_adomain: [],
    hb_bidder: [],
    hb_bidder_medianet: [],
    hb_crid: [],
    hb_crid_medianet: [],
    hb_format: [],
    hb_format_medianet: [],
    hb_pb: [],
    hb_pb_medianet: [],
    hb_size: [],
    hb_size_medianet: [],
    hb_source: [],
    hb_source_medianet: [],
    mnadc: ["div-test-1"]
  },
  div-test-2: {
    hb_adid: ["71a81eb115170d8"],
    hb_adid_medianet: ["71a81eb115170d8"],
    hb_adomain: [],
    hb_bidder: ["medianet"],
    hb_bidder_medianet: ["medianet"],
    hb_crid: [],
    hb_crid_medianet: [],
    hb_format: ["banner"],
    hb_format_medianet: ["banner"],
    hb_pb: ["15.00"],
    hb_pb_medianet: ["15.00"],
    hb_size: ["300x250"],
    hb_size_medianet: ["300x250"],
    hb_source: [],
    hb_source_medianet: [],
    mnadc: ["div-test-2"]
  }
}
```

**Reason:** 

* Upon **setTargetingForGPTAsync(adUnitCode)**, prebid retrieves both bidderSettings and RTD targetings, combines them, filters null targetings, and updates targeting map of google slots.
* While retrieving RTD targetings, prebid retrieves all adUnit targetings that have been auctioned via prebid in all auctions on the page. (https://github.com/prebid/Prebid.js/blob/master/src/targeting.js#L716 )
* This causes targetings of **all previously auctioned** slots to also get updated.

body:
<!--
Thank you for your pull request! 

Please title your pull request like this: 'Module: Change', eg 'Fraggles Bid Adapter: support fragglerock'

-->

## Type of change
<!-- Remove items that don't apply and/or select an item by changing [ ] to [x] -->
- [ ] Bugfix


## Description of change
While using mergeDeep, when the source is undefined, the same value is getting copied into a valid target value.
For eg: consider the following objects:
let payload = {
   user: {
      gender: 'M',
      yob: 1990,
      eids: [
      {
         source: "criteo.com",
         uids: [{id: "abcd", atype: 1}]
      }]
   }
}
let user: { // comes from fpd data
   keywords: "p_standard=adv",
   eids: undefined
}

mergeDeep(payload, user) results into following object:
{
   eids: undefined,
   gender: "M",
   keywords: "p_standard=adv",
   yob: 1990
}

Be sure to test the integration with your adserver using the [Hello World](https://github.com/prebid/Prebid.js/blob/master/integrationExamples/gpt/hello_world.html) sample page. -->
